articles.title: Toronto's Hope House offers women path out of homelessness. But the city's far from supportive housing goal
articles.summary: A year after St. Felix Centre in downtown Toronto opened Hope House, the rent-geared-to-income supportive housing residence says it's making a difference in the lives of women and children experiencing housing instability, and domestic violence. It's part of the city's plan to create more affordable and long-term housing solutions, as many people are still being turned away from shelters every mon

articles.title: Woman arrested for impaired driving after flipping car on Don Valley Parkway
articles.summary: A woman was arrested for impaired driving after losing control of her vehicle and flipping it over on a downtown Toronto highway on Saturday afternoon. Emergency crews were called to the southbound ramp of the Don Valley Parkway that leads to the westbound Gardiner Expressway just before 1:30 p.m. for reports of a single-vehicle collision. […]

articles.title: Blue Jays unveil Joe Carter statue outside Rogers Centre honouring World Series championships
articles.summary: A statue honouring the 1992 and 1993 World Series champion Toronto Blue Jays has been unveiled outside of Rogers Centre.

articles.title: Woman dead following stabbing at North York building: Toronto police
articles.summary: Toronto police say a woman is dead after a stabbing at a building in North York Saturday afternoon.

articles.title: Blue Jays unveil statue outside Rogers Centre honouring World Series championships
articles.summary: A statue honouring the 1992 and 1993 World Series champion Toronto Blue Jays has been unveiled outside of Rogers Centre. It shows right-fielder Joe Carter mid-jump as he rounds the bases following his walk-off home run to win the 1993 World Series. The statue is located at the southeast corner of the ballpark, outside Gate […]

articles.title: Woman killed in North York stabbing, male suspect at large
articles.summary: A woman was killed during a stabbing in North York on Saturday afternoon, according to Toronto police. Officers were called to a high-rise residential building near Fairview Mall Drive and Don Mills Road just after 2:30 p.m. and found a woman with stab wounds in the hallway of building. Police say the woman is in […]

articles.title: 2 charged in Toronto police distraction theft probe after grandmother, child allegedly targeted
articles.summary: Toronto police officers allege the incident happened on the morning of May 18 near Culford Road and Falstaff Avenue.

articles.title: Severe thunderstorm watches, earlier tornado warnings across Greater Toronto Area dropped
articles.summary: Environment and Climate Change Canada meteorologists say the Greater Toronto Area could also see hail and up to 40 mm. of rain.

articles.title: Peel police identify 17-year-old brothers as suspects in fatal Brampton shooting
articles.summary: Peel police have identified two teenage brothers as suspects in a fatal shooting in Brampton last week.

articles.title: Metrolinx cleaning chemical spill in east-end creek related to Scarborough subway extension work
articles.summary: City says it was notified of spill on July 8. Metrolinx says the spill is contained and a specialized team is working to remove "polymer-based slurry" from creek.

articles.title: Mosquitoes in Toronto, York and Halton test positive for West Nile virus
articles.summary: A batch of mosquitoes in Toronto has tested positive for West Nile virus, Toronto Public Health says.
